title: FABLE (first-wave PRIORITY): optimal-resource-usage plan — work taxonomy + architecture reuse + cross-experiment GPU-result reuse
description:
Maintainer-designated PRIORITY (Jesse, 2026-07-09): when Fable is back online, one of its FIRST tasks is to produce a concrete OPTIMAL-RESOURCE-USAGE plan (deliverable: docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md + integration into the research-engine framework + roles/practices). Do this EARLY — before large new experiment spend — because it optimizes all downstream work. Three required components:

(1) WORK TAXONOMY (Opus vs Fable boundary, made precise). Define, with a concrete decision rubric, exactly which work is ROUTINE / non-complex and can be done by cheaper models (Opus execution) WITHOUT reducing the quality or potential of experiments, vs which is complex/creative/definitional and must stay Fable. Refine the existing Fable-designs / Opus-runs split (docs/next/opus-execution-practices.md, research-engine.md §3, .claude/agents/experiment-runner.md + kb-pipeline-runner.md) into a checklist/flowchart an agent can apply per task. Goal: maximise cheap-model throughput with zero quality loss; flag any grey zones as forks.

(2) ARCHITECTURE REUSE + COMPOSITION. Identify where architectures (kernel / encoder / model components) can be RE-USED and PLUGGED TOGETHER in different combinations to test architectural permutations efficiently — where the existing work to build ONE kernel can be reused to build a SIMILAR kernel (e.g. shared basis, shared minting, shared corpora, shared adapters), and where components compose so a permutation grid is covered by a small set of reusable building blocks rather than N bespoke builds. Connect to the idea-registry slots/permutations (registry/ideas.jsonl), the architecture ladder (docs/next/architecture-ladder.md), and the kernel-variant expansion split.

(3) CROSS-EXPERIMENT GPU-RESULT REUSE. Identify where LOGGED training/eval outputs from one GPU run can be REUSED across multiple experiments/analyses instead of re-running — i.e. bake in the 're-analyse before re-running' principle (the F2 -> f2b lesson: gap-closure was a free re-analysis over logged data). Define a results/artifact ledger + reuse rules: which arms/checkpoints/decodes are reusable across experiments, and a gate that forces a check for an existing logged result before authorising any new GPU spend.

Output: the plan doc + concrete mechanisms (rubric, reusable-component map, results-reuse ledger + pre-spend check) folded into the engine so they are enforced, not advisory.
notes: REFINEMENT (Jesse, 2026-07-09): do NOT frame components (2) architecture-reuse and (3) GPU-result-reuse as retroactive gates ONLY. ALSO add a PROACTIVE component (4): produce a reuse-MAXIMISING ORDERING/prioritisation of pending work — sequence experiments so broadly-reusable artefacts are produced first and shared arms/checkpoints/corpora are built once and consumed many times, rather than only checking for reuse at spend time. Opus has already done the PLANNING-STEP-NOW reconnaissance the maintainer requested: docs/next/reuse-ordering-analysis.md (SPECULATIVE/uncommitted-then-committed; Opus recon, NOT a decision) — it maps dependency edges, shared pins, logged GPU outputs, and proposes a draft wave ordering (Wave 1 zero-GPU: l3a-parse/a5-nl/g5/g4+build d-ax; then POST-F2-INFRA decision; then e9-full→e9-c shared-producer-first; efficiency f-track; f7 last consuming survivors' decodes; family-h0→a-h0). Fable OWNS validating/rejecting/re-deriving this ordering and every 'reuse is licensed' call — esp. the load-bearing honesty question (§5): whether pre-registration discipline permits an experiment to consume another record's logged per-item decodes as its own arm output WITHOUT a fresh run (f2b was a replication, not pure reuse). Decide whether this folds into the backlog L-score (research-engine.md §2.7) or stands as a separate ordering.

title: Kernel construction methodology: how should canonical concept representations actually be built?
description: @jeswr (2026-07-07) stepped back from the identity/reconciliation question to the FOUNDATIONAL one: is LLM-generation (Haiku emitting NSM explications) even the right way to construct the kernel? Wikipedia-sourcing was 'a whim'. Candidate alternatives to research + test: (a) LLM-generated NSM explications (current); (b) DICTIONARY-GRAPH + convergence — turn each dictionary definition into relations, run a convergence algorithm to a fixpoint (mirrors the published 'grounding kernel' / minimum-grounding-set work of Vincent-Lamarre/Masse/Harnad 'The Latent Structure of Dictionaries', and Formal Concept Analysis); (c) EXTRACT FROM MODEL INTERNALS — 'deconstruct a local model so its output layer matches the construction we want' (SAE/dictionary-learning/interp; connects to this project's E8 kernel<->SAE alignment result); (d) principled hybrids. Concern: an LLM 'just spits out what it thinks things should be like' — may be circular/ungrounded/non-canonical.
design: Deep-research workflow w7tu4h3kr (5 Fable angles + synthesis): decompositional-semantics theory (is a canonical primitive basis real? NSM vs Fodor's atomism); dictionary-graphs/grounding-kernels (Harnad et al; FCA); symbol-grounding/circularity (Harnad SGP; distributional vs decompositional; is a definition-only kernel grounded?); model-internal extraction (SAE/monosemanticity/linear-rep; canonical? deterministic? training-free?); LLM-generator critique (proposer-then-verified vs source-of-truth; determinism from a stochastic generator). Deliverable: theory-grounded recommendation + honest uncertainty map + a SEQUENCED experimental programme (cheapest/most-decision-relevant first) to empirically discriminate the approaches. E10-fix (order-invariance of reconciliation) becomes ONE rung, downstream of choosing the substrate.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Theory-grounded recommended construction approach (or principled hybrid) with reasoning. 2) Explicit uncertainty map (settled-by-literature vs open-empirical vs philosophical). 3) A proper sequenced set of experiments, each with question/method/pass-fail/cost/decision-unblocked. 4) Clear verdict on whether LLM-generation is legitimate as a verified-proposer vs circular as source-of-truth.

title: FABLE DESIGN + sign-off: two maintainer-proposed I/O-compression kernel ideas (idea-crosslingual-phrase-coverage-io-compression, idea-compositional-rollup-invented-concepts)
description:
Jesse 2026-07-09 proposed two EFFICIENCY-thesis compression ideas, registered in registry/ideas.jsonl (status: proposed, premise_eligible: false, EXTRAPOLATION). EXPLICIT: do NOT build yet — Fable must create AND sign off a decisive experiment design first (this is a Fable design task, not Opus execution).

(1) idea-crosslingual-phrase-coverage-io-compression: mint concepts for the highest-frequency words/phrases ACROSS languages (statistical selection) so the encoder compresses LLM input into few concept tokens + the decoder expresses high-bandwidth content in few tokens; generate definitions cheaply via a Haiku concept-definer with a fully CACHED prefix and only the single word as variable input (tiny, ideally symbolic, output). FORK Jesse flagged: does asking for symbolic output cut tokens or INCREASE Haiku thinking-time? cheap decisive sub-test first. Generates an ALTERNATIVE kernel.

(2) idea-compositional-rollup-invented-concepts: encoder does static/symbolic roll-up of 'a is b' / 'a AND b' / 'a OR c' structure into a single INVENTED composite concept (vector deterministically derived via the existing Hadamard+HRR binding algebra) so those tokens reach the LLM as one concept; decoder gets a per-utterance invented-concept dictionary for lossless expansion.

Design constraints for Fable: BOTH must beat the kernel-as-text null (Law 2) and survive the trained-bridge overhead (Law 1); confront the LCM/CALM fixed-semantic-IO scaling penalty (phi-fixedness axis); and — critically — idea (2) is constrained by idea-ast-input-injection-boundary (X1): frame on the COMPRESSION/EFFICIENCY endpoint, NEVER as accuracy-from-structure (which X1 showed is parity + non-transferring). Pre-register with a cost/accuracy Pareto metric vector before any spend.

title: Order-invariant kernel reconciliation: dependency-aware re-derivation + bidirectional co-definition
description: Concepts are minted in frequency-rank order, but definitional dependency does NOT follow frequency. A concept defined early (e.g. 'male') may be expressible more concisely/faithfully once a higher-level concept (e.g. 'person') is later available as a molecule — yet there is NO reconciliation pass that revisits earlier concepts to re-express them over later-minted higher-level concepts. Consequence: the kernel's conciseness AND canonicity depend on mint order, so two corpora built in different orders would differ. That contradicts the core claim of canonical, deterministic concept vectors — canonicity requires order-invariance (a fixpoint). Raised by @jeswr 2026-07-07. DEFERRED to a Claude-Fable session (design work; no Fable access now).
design: Two coupled sub-problems. (1) Dependency-aware re-derivation: detect concepts whose explication shortens/improves by referencing a now-available higher-level molecule, and re-derive them. Content-addressing (urn:kot: = hash of explication) means re-derivation CHANGES the hash -> need either (a) versioning/migration (old->new urn, rewrite referrers) or (b) settle-to-fixpoint-THEN-freeze: iterate re-derivation on mutable IDs to convergence, content-address only once stable. (2) Bidirectional/mutually-referential cycles: buy<->sell, parent<->child, left<->right, cause<->effect, borrow<->lend — each naturally defined via the other, but rank-order forces one first (bare primes) and the other gets the concise cross-ref. Options: co-definition per strongly-connected component, or a graph fixpoint. Procedure: build dependency graph; compute SCCs; topologically re-derive across SCCs, jointly solve within each; iterate to fixpoint; THEN content-address. Ties to E9: a reconciled kernel should be MORE order-invariant; measure vector drift pre/post.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Reconciliation pass yields an order-invariant settled corpus (same fixpoint regardless of mint order; verify with two random-order subsets). 2) Bidirectional pairs handled by co-definition, not first-mover verbosity. 3) Migration/versioning story for content-addresses. 4) Measure avg explication size + order-invariance before/after.

title: X2-corpus FAIL 51/54: decoder drops optional SP slots / misorders referent intro under deep quote+op nesting (afraid, angry, sad)
notes:
Found by kernel-v0 corpus re-run (kernel-of-truth-138; poc/results/x2-kernel-v0-report.md). afraid: hard fail ERR_REF_NOT_INTRODUCED (ref 2 introduced out of order inside quote). angry: decoded AST drops "quant":"SOME" on an SP inside a quote (ok-but-wrong, minConfidence 0, step 'atomic-forced'). sad: drops an entire undergoer SP inside quote>NOT>CAN>DO nesting. IDENTICAL failures with minimal (referenced-ids-only) and full-corpus cleanup lexicons => NOT lexicon interference; it is presence-detection SNR under deep quote nesting. All 18 reference-bearing concepts decode exactly; failures are the quote-re-anchored emotion trio. Synthetic X2 gate (720/720 full grid) never generates these shapes — extend the generator or add authored-corpus regression fixtures.
Root-caused (decode-spec-fix agent): SPEC RULING — the afraid RECORD IS LEGAL and the VALIDATOR IS RIGHT per gist §4.2 ('exactly one introducing occurrence'; 'a cdef:ref to an undeclared or not-yet-introduced index is a gate error'; 'Quote-local referents (bound inside a cdef:quoteClauses) scope to that quote'): ref 2 is declared, bound exactly once in quote clause 0, and every mention follows it inside the same quote. The decoder never disagreed about the RULE — it DROPPED the introducing SP (decoded HAPPEN.roles={}), then failed its own output's validation. Same mechanism as angry/sad: at quote>op depth the true optional SP's presence signature (0.0335) sits BELOW pure-noise slots at the same depth (0.036-0.040) vs the 5-sigma gate 0.0552 — locally unseparable. FIX: decoder residual-completion phase (encoder/src/decoder.ts): after convergence, re-encode the decoded AST, subtract from v; missed content is then nearly the only residual content (measured 6-30x SNR at the missed sites: head 0.35-0.67, quant 0.33-0.68, bind ref:2 0.31, temporal op 0.46 vs noise ~0.02); probe every empty optional site there and accept a candidate only if the re-encoded edited clause explains the restored residual strictly better (exact matched-filter competition; phantoms cannot win). Encoder content-hash UNCHANGED (40e8c8ba...e2d10c); corpus UNCHANGED (no re-authoring, corpus hash stays f23f5211...); unit suite 38/38 (5 new regression tests incl. both validator ruling directions, encoder/test/corpus-regression.test.ts).
close_reason: X2-corpus re-run PASS: minimal 54/54 AND full-corpus 54/54 exact (was 51/54 both; report x2-kernel-v0-report.md 2026-07-07T04:22Z). Ruling: afraid record LEGAL, validator right (gist §4.2); decoder fixed via residual-completion phase. Encoder content-hash unchanged (40e8c8ba...e2d10c), corpus untouched (hash f23f5211... unchanged, no X1-corpus re-run needed), unit suite 38/38. Follow-ups filed: synthetic-gate re-run/generator coverage (P2), substitution/removal completion (P3).

comments.text: E8 harness READY-TO-RUN (run gated on coordinator spend approval). Design pinned in poc/e8/README.md BEFORE build. Sources (both ungated, HF-revision-pinned): gpt2 + jbloom/GPT2-Small-SAEs-Reformatted blocks.6.hook_resid_pre (L1 ReLU, d_sae 24576, 151MB) and EleutherAI/pythia-160m + sae-pythia-160m-32k layers.5 (TopK k=32, 32768 latents, 201MB); both = residual between blocks 5/6 = hidden_states[6] = L/2. Gemma Scope rejected for primary pair (base model gated). Item set = 51 E2 analysis items; molecules EXCLUDED (no derived vectors; task premise 54+108 corrected to 54+54 in README). Operationalisation: mean per-token SAE-feature activation over E2 context banks -> within-family cosine RDMs -> cross-family correspondence X via masked profile-Spearman; primary P1 = Mantel/shuffled-kernel on K_jl512 vs X_sym p<0.01, co-primary P2 = partial | orig3; kernel-free gate G first. Controls all green: planted-correspondence PASS, structured-null NULL (gate passes, kernel signal absent), pure-noise PRECONDITION FAIL; mock e2e + stubbed-Modal wiring green (bash poc/e8/validate.sh). RUN: poc/modal/.venv/bin/modal run poc/modal/modal_e8.py (T4, ~1.28GB to kot-hf-cache volume, est $0.06-0.19 cold / $0.03-0.10 warm; envelope was $5-15) then python3 poc/e8/analyze.py poc/e8/results-incoming/<stamp>-modal (~25 min niced CPU). Weakest link (pinned, README §6): the concept->feature mapping routes through the exponent WORD in synthetic contexts — the SAE never sees the explication; orig3/emb4 partials are the only guard against word-relatedness carrying the result.

title: Decide: molecule/haiku grounding-ref substitution vs stable placeholder ids (generation 2)
description: Generation-1 minting kept molecules-v0 and haiku-tier in stable-ref mode: grounding refs (mostly cross-corpus into kernel-v0, embedded in authored prose as {urn:...|gloss} tokens) were NOT rewritten to minted urn:kot: URNs. Rationale: task rule 'cross-corpus refs stay annotations/never rewritten into payloads' + do-not-alter-authored-bytes + the gist s3.5 1024-byte NFC grounding-note cap (substitution grows notes ~48 bytes/ref). Tension: gist s3.5 rule 4 wants grounding refs to be FINAL URNs inside the hash. Decide before generation 2; if substitution wins, specify the cap semantics on the resolved form and re-mint both corpora.

title: Bulk-corpus READMEs: document the minted identity surface (sourceId INSIDE identity)
description: The mint pass (kernel-of-truth-mud) put the stable sourceId INSIDE every bulk-extracted corpus's identity payload — required empirically: with the lexical-wn31 README's literal 'inside identity' list ({schema, semanticStatus, pos, ssType, axioms}, no offset), 66,569 of 117,791 synsets collapse to shared URNs (one group of 3,620 axiom-less adjectives) — an unsound merge of distinct concepts. The manifests' minting.identityPayload blocks document the actual splits; the READMEs of lexical-wn31 (and the other bulk corpora) should be updated to match, citing design-bulk-kernel.md ('a bulk record is an assertion by an extractor about a source').

title: E8 feature-level correspondence variant (independent corpus)
description: poc/e8 tests CONCEPT-level correspondence; a feature-level test through the same 51 concept probes is degenerate (target and predictor are functions of the same activation profiles — README §6.4). Non-circular version: establish cross-family FEATURE correspondence on an independent shared text corpus (e.g. a fixed 10-50k-sentence slice, activation-profile correlation, the universality-literature method), then test whether kernel coordinates of concept-anchored features predict which features correspond. Bigger extraction (~GPU-hours not minutes); design doc first.

title: E8 third family: Gemma Scope (gated) for a second family-pair
description: poc/e8 primary pair is gpt2 + pythia-160m (both ungated). Gemma Scope (google/gemma-scope-2b-pt-res, ungated SAEs; suggest layer 12 width 16k canonical-L0 params.npz, JumpReLU) is the flagship suite and the natural third family, but base model google/gemma-2-2b is gated=manual: needs coordinator HF account licence acceptance + HF_TOKEN as a Modal secret. A second pair would let E8 report a >=2-of-N quantifier instead of the single-pair scope limit (poc/e8/README.md §6.2). JumpReLU encode = act * (act > threshold) with params.npz keys W_enc/b_enc/W_dec/b_dec/threshold — add arch 'gemma_jumprelu' to e8_runner.SAEDict.

Once derived: extend poc/e8/build_inputs.py item set + author bank assignments for molecule words (mostly noun bank; judgement to be pinned), and re-run. Depends conceptually on the encoder follow-up named in design-molecule-tier.md.

title: onto-sumo: extract SUMO domain .kif files beyond Merge+MILO
description: onto-sumo v0.1 extracted the definitional core (Merge.kif + Mid-level-ontology.kif, 15,595 axioms). SUMO has ~100 domain .kif files (Cars, Hotel, Medicine, Geography, ...) at github.com/ontologyportal/sumo. The kot-sumo/1 KIF parser+extractor handles them unchanged (add files to FILES[] with sha pins; owner/subject logic is file-agnostic). Domain files add term coverage but are less definitional (mostly instance/subclass facts). Do as demand warrants; note the one dangling target (FabricIron -> HouseUtilityAppliance) resolves once HouseholdAppliances.kif is added.

title: Assess + mine DOLCE / gUFO (foundational ontologies) into an onto-dolce tier
description: The claim bead title also named DOLCE/gUFO; the mining directive scoped OBO/SUMO/FrameNet, so DOLCE/gUFO deferred here. gUFO (github.com/nemo-ufes/gufo) is directly relevant: kernel-v0 already targets gufo:Event and gufo:Kind, and onto-obo's alignment bridges to BFO (gUFO-adjacent). gUFO is OWL (Turtle) - would need a small Turtle/OWL reader (the programme avoided hand-rolled RDF/XML for BFO; Turtle is more tractable). DOLCE (DOLCE-Lite OWL, or the Loux/CLIF form) is the other classic upper ontology. Value: sharpen the continuant/occurrent + endurant/perdurant + rigid-sortal (Kind) alignments that kernel-v0's gUFO commitments depend on. Check licences (gUFO CC BY; DOLCE per ISTC-CNR terms).

math-mm: grammar-parse dependency refinement
description: Current dependency semantics is token-level (stated rule in data/math-mm/extractor/extract.mjs header + README). Documented precision limits found in the 100-record review: (1) punctuation attribution - '(' -> wi, ',' -> wif, braces -> cab; (2) pattern-reuse formers (csn, cpr, w3o, cv, ...) invisible inside definientia (e.g. df-tp lacks a df-sn dep); (3) cv unreachable. Fix requires parsing each statement against set.mm's (unambiguous) grammar to get parse-tree deps. Deterministic, self-contained upgrade; re-extraction discipline applies (new extractorHash, re-run all gates).

title: math-mm: mint content-addressed ids (RDF record form for pm-mm/1 + concept-hash pipeline over the 2998 records)

Design-math-sector.md SS3.5 specifies how profile-M plugs into the gist SS6 hash machinery; pm-mm/1 needs its RDF record form + profile bundle header before real urn:concept: ids can be minted. title: kernel-v0 ontology follow-up: concepts inside/near duplicate primes INSIDE/NEAR (sense-identical in 50/50 sampled uses each) and label 'kind' collides with dominant English 'nice' sense (49/50) — consider rename/alias/drop at the next kernel version bump (touches pre-reg-pinned manifest = new pre-registration, NOT an amendment; measured alias frequencies in m0a-shadowed-policy.md section 2b)
Decoder residual completion is insertion-only: extend the global matched-filter competition to SUBSTITUTION/REMOVAL of wrong optional content
notes: The kernel-of-truth-0kn fix recovers MISSED optional content from the global residual (v minus re-encoded decode) via insertion candidates accepted only when the re-encoded edit explains the restored residual strictly better. ok-but-wrong decodes where content was decoded WRONG (wrong atom/quant/phantom optional slot) leave a +true/-decoded signature in the same residual and are amenable to the same acceptance test with substitution/removal candidate generation (probe filled optional sites for a strictly-better replacement, incl. dropping the filler). No known current failure needs it (X2-corpus 54/54 target after 0kn) — this is robustness for future corpora.

title: kernel-v0: nearest pair is the emotion trio (afraid<->sad 0.116 rad), not the deliberately-near give/take/gift cluster
notes: X1-corpus (poc/results/x1-kernel-v0-report.md, kernel-of-truth-138): all-pairs minimum is afraid<->sad at 0.1156 rad (cos 0.9933), then happy<->sad 0.2051, afraid<->happy 0.2238; the authored-to-be-near possession-transfer cluster first appears at rank 4 (repair<->take 0.4884) and give/take/gift themselves are not in the top 10 at all (top10 in the report json). The emotion trio shares nearly all clause structure (feel + quote re-anchoring), differing only in small deep-quote fragments that the encoder attenuates. Implication for corpus authoring: structural overlap dominates geometric proximity, not intended semantic nearness — worth a corpus README note and maybe a v0.2 authoring pass if margin work wants give/take to be the stress pair.
